## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On April 7, 1948, the World Health Organization (WHO) was officially and aggressively established as a massive, specialized agency of the United Nations. Headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland, it was heavily tasked with achieving the absolute highest possible level of health for all global populations. The massive organization played an absolute, foundational role in orchestrating the massive, historic eradication of smallpox and aggressively coordinating global responses to massive pandemics, heavily cementing its status as the absolute bedrock of global public health.

On April 7, 1994, one of the most horrific, massive, and deeply traumatic atrocities of the late 20th century officially commenced: the Rwandan Genocide. Following the aggressive assassination of President Juvénal Habyarimana, Hutu extremist militias, heavily backed by the state, unleashed a massive, incredibly brutal campaign of ethnic cleansing. Over the next 100 days, an estimated 800,000 Tutsis and moderate Hutus were aggressively slaughtered, deeply exposing the massive, catastrophic failure of the international community and UN peacekeepers to aggressively intervene.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On April 7, 1875, Swami Dayananda Saraswati officially and aggressively founded the massive, highly influential Arya Samaj in Bombay (Mumbai). The massive Hindu reform movement heavily advocated for a strict, aggressive return to the absolute authority of the infallible Vedas. By fiercely rejecting massive idolatry, the oppressive caste system, and untouchability, the Arya Samaj played an incredibly massive, foundational role in modernizing Hindu society and aggressively fueling the spiritual and cultural awakening that heavily underpinned the Indian independence movement.

On April 7, 1920, Ravi Shankar, the absolute, legendary, and globally renowned Indian musician and composer, was born in Varanasi. A massive, unparalleled virtuoso of the sitar, he aggressively exported Indian classical music to the Western world. His brilliant, massive collaborations with iconic musicians like George Harrison of The Beatles and violinist Yehudi Menuhin fundamentally popularized Indian ragas globally, securing his legacy as the most massive, culturally influential Indian musical ambassador of the 20th century.
